06 April 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Trump, Hegseth, Caine Laud Successful Rescue of Downed Airmen in Iran DoD News 06 Apr 2026 -- During a White House press conference today, President Donald J. Trump, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Air Force Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, lauded the military's success in a harrowing Easter weekend rescue of two airmen.
  • Strikes Hit Iranian Petrochemical Facilities In South Pars RFE/RL 06 Apr 2026 -- Israeli strikes hit petrochemical facilities in southern Iran on April 6, Iranian media reported, with damage reported but no casualties.
  • Another Iranian petrochemical plant bombed in US-Israeli aggression Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Oil Ministry says US-Israeli attacks have targeted petrochemical complexes in the southern Iranian province of Bushehr, just days after similar plants came under attack in the southwestern province of Khuzestan.
  • Head Of Revolutionary Guards Intelligence Organization Killed RFE/RL 06 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) says that the head of its Intelligence Organization, Brigadier General Seyed Majid Khademi, has been killed.
  • Iran says 24 pharma, medical units damaged in US-Israeli assaults Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Food and Drug Administration (IFDA) says 24 pharmaceutical and medical-equipment facilities across multiple provinces have been damaged in US-Israeli aggression, though officials report no disruption to the national supply of essential medicines.
  • IRGC intelligence chief martyred in Israeli strike on Tehran Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The head of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Intelligence Organization has been martyred in an Israeli airstrike on the Iranian capital of Tehran, amid the unprovoked US-Israeli aggression against the country.

  • 'Northern cities of occupied territories abandoned' as missiles freely hit targets: IRGC commander Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The Zionist regime has effectively conceded the collapse of its defense by abandoning its northern settlements, a senior Iranian military commander said, as Tehran's missiles continue to hit strategic targets deep inside the occupied territories with no sign of interception.
  • IRGC shoots down two intruding hostile drones in southern Iran Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) air defense units have intercepted and destroyed two enemy drones, as they were flying in the skies over the Persian Gulf island of Qeshm and the southern province of Khuzestan.
  • Iran army launches drone attacks on two US bases in Kuwait Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Iran's army has launched retaliatory drone attacks on Kuwait's al-Kharj Base and al-Adiri Camp which hosts strategic US aircraft refueling planes.
  • Only Iran can stop 'domino of fire' engulfing entire region: IRGC aerospace chief Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The Aerospace Force of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says a "domino of fire" has begun across the region and that only Iran is in a position to stop itit.
  • Four Israeli settlers killed in Iranian missile strike on Haifa Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- At least four Israeli settlers have been killed and several others injured as missiles fired by Iran in retaliation for the ongoing US-Israeli war of aggression against the Islamic Republic hit targets in the southern occupied lands.
  • IRGC strikes US, Israeli military-industrial sites in wave 98 of Operation True Promise 4 Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has launched the 98th wave of the decisive Operation True Promise 4, targeting command, operational and logistics centers, as well as military-industrial infrastructure used by the United States and Israel in the region.
  • War to continue until enemy is brought to 'point of genuine remorse': Army spox Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The spokesman for the Iranian Army said on Sunday that the retaliatory operations against the ongoing US-Israeli aggression will continue until the enemy is brought to a "point of genuine remorse," noting that such an outcome is a strategic necessity.

  • In joint operation with IRGC, resistance forces, Yemeni army targets Israeli positions Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The spokesperson for the Yemeni armed forces says the country's military units, in conjunction with Iran's Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) and regional resistance groups, have carried out a series of missile and drone strikes against strategic sites deep inside the occupied territories.

  • Trump Issues Stark "One Night" Warning to Iran, Touts Daring Rescue RFE/RL 06 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump renewed his threat to wipe out Iran's power plants and bridges if it doesn't reach an agreement to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz, warning the country could be "taken out in one night."
  • Amid Slew Of Threats, Trump Extends Deadline For Iran To Open Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 06 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump repeated his threat to strike Iranian infrastructure hard if Tehran does not open the Strait of Hormuz, using explosive language hours after announcing that US forces rescued the missing crew member from a downed F-15 fighter jet.
  • Trump hints at extending deadline for Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz Global Times 06 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump posted "Tuesday, 8:00 p.m. Eastern Time!" on social media on Sunday, a message that appeared to signal a possible extension of the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
  • 'Mentally unbalanced': US politicians condemn Trump's 'evil' threats against Iran Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Many US political figures have sharply criticized President Donald Trump following his expletive-laden threats aimed at trying to force Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, casting his mental stability into doubt and highlighting the risks posed to American troops, with some even calling for his removal.

  • Prime Minister Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 06 Apr 2026 -- "Yesterday, I spoke again with my friend, President Trump. I expressed our immense appreciation for the heroic rescue operation of that American aircrew member"

  • Trump's 'delusional' threats will not make up for US humiliation in West Asia: Iran military Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump's "baseless threats" cannot erase Washington's humiliation in West Asia, Iran's senior military spokesman said, dismissing the latest American ultimatum as a sign of deadlock and desperation.
  • 'Heavy bombs' won't halt nuclear tech progress: Iran atomic agency Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The US-Israeli attack on Iran's yellowcake plant in Ardakan is a flagrant violation of international law, but it will never stop the Islamic Republic's peaceful nuclear program, the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) said in a statement.
  • FM Araghchi condemns US-Israeli barbaric attack on 'MIT of Iran' Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has condemned the latest criminal US-Israeli coordinated strike on Sharif University of Technology, one of Iran's most prestigious and advanced centers of engineering, science, and innovation, widely regarded as the country's equivalent of MIT.
  • Assassinations will not stop Iranian armed forces: Leader Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei has extended condolences to Iranian military commanders over the martyrdom of the head of the Intelligence Organization of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC).
  • Iran says non-hostile countries can coordinate access to Strait of Hormuz Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Iran says that the countries that are not hostile to the Islamic Republic can secure the safe passage of their vessels through the Strait of Hormuz via "diplomatic channels."
  • Iran's atomic chief denounces US-Israeli attacks on nuclear plants as 'war crime' Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The head of the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) has stated that the latest US-Israeli attack on the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant amounts to a war crime, stressing that the failure of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) to condemn such attacks has emboldened the aggressors.
  • 'Sprit of resistance will continue': Iran officials condemn Israeli assassination of IRGC intel. chief Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Messages of condolences pour in after the head of the Intelligence Organization of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC), Major General Majid Khademi, was assassinated in a US-Israeli terror strike in Tehran.
  • Iran says US-proposed ceasefire 'just a pause before renewed crimes' Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Iran rejects the US's self-proclaimed proposals for a ceasefire, warning that any pauses resulting from such a ceasefire would only allow adversaries to regroup and commit further crimes against the Iranian nation.
  • Iran's military vows crushing response to US-Israeli attacks on civilian facilities Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The spokesperson for the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters has vowed that any repetition of acts of aggression against Iranian civilian facilities will draw a much harsher and far more extensive retaliatory operation by the country's Armed Forces.
  • Ramifications of attacks on infrastructure won't be limited to Iran, deputy FM warns Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- A senior Iranian diplomat warns that the consequences of US President Donald Trump's threats against Iran's infrastructure will not be confined to the Islamic Republic alone, advising him to stop such rhetoric.
  • Iran slams US-Israeli attack on heavy water facility as 'crime against human health' Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) says US-Israeli attack on Iran's heavy water facility is a crime against science and human health, vowing to continue the path of scientific progress despite the aggression.
  • US-Israeli attack targets Iran's Sharif University of Technology, causing damage Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The US-Israeli war coalition has conducted an attack on Sharif University of Technology in Tehran, in recurring strikes against Iran's academic and civilian infrastructure.
  • Iran Prepares Response to Mediators' Ceasefire Proposals - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 06 Apr 2026 -- Iran has prepared a response to the proposals of the mediators on a ceasefire with the United States, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei said on Monday.
  • Iran Tells IAEA Shelling of Bushehr NPP Is War Crime Example Sputnik 06 Apr 2026 -- The shelling of the Bushehr nuclear power plant (NPP) is an example of a war crime, and Iran has sent a letter of protest to International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Grossi, demanding that he condemn such actions, the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran said on Monday.

  • Head Of UN Nuclear Watchdog Says Strikes Near Bushehr Plant 'Must Stop' RFE/RL 06 Apr 2026 -- The director of the International Atomic Energy Agency, Rafael Grossi, has warned that military strikes near Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant pose a "very real danger to nuclear safety."

  • China willing to cooperate with Russia to de-escalate Mideast situation, says FM Global Times 06 Apr 2026 -- China is willing to continue working with Russia within the framework of the United Nations (UN) Security Council, maintain timely communication on major issues, and make joint efforts to help de-escalate the situation in the Middle East, safeguard regional peace and stability, and uphold common security for the world, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Sunday.

  • US-Israeli aggression against Iran has set entire region 'on fire': Kremlin Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- The Kremlin has warned that the ongoing US-Israeli war of aggression against Iran has engulfed the entire Persian Gulf and West Asia, setting the region "on fire" with dangerous and far-reaching consequences.

  • Japan-Iran Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 06 Apr 2026
  • Japan-Pakistan Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 06 Apr 2026
  • Palace: Hormuz access assures oil supply; eyes options vs. hoarding Philippine News Agency 06 Apr 2026 -- Malacañang on Monday said efforts to secure safe passage for Philippine-bound vessels through the Strait of Hormuz are critical to ensuring uninterrupted fuel supply, even as it signaled openness to proposals such as temporary fuel purchase limits to curb hoarding.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Joint Task Force Commits Over $600 Million to Procure New Counter-UAS Capability DoD News 06 Apr 2026 -- Joint Interagency Task Force 401 has committed over $600 million at a record pace to strengthen counter-unmanned aircraft systems capabilities in support of Operation Epic Fury, the FIFA World Cup and homeland defense. This critical milestone demonstrates significant progress in counter-drone efforts and interagency collaboration.
  • DLA Energy's Supply Chain Fuels Artemis II Mission DoD News 06 Apr 2026 -- When NASA's Artemis II mission launched April 1, its success relied on a complex and critical supply chain managed by the Defense Logistics Agency Energy.
  • USS John P. Murtha to support NASA's Artemis II mission US Navy 06 Apr 2026 -- Amphibious transport dock ship USS John P. Murtha (LPD 26) is slated to serve as the recovery ship for the Orion spacecraft and its crew upon their return from the historic Artemis II mission.
  • USS Ashland Completes Ship Wartime Repair and Maintenance in the Philippines US Navy 06 Apr 2026 -- Whidbey Island-class amphibious dock landing ship USS Ashland (LSD 48) completed a ship wartime repair and maintenance exercise (SWARMEX), April 5, as part of its scheduled port visit to Cebu.

  • German Eurofighters hand over NATO's Enhanced Air Policing Mission in Romania to the Royal Air Force NATO Allied Air Command 06 Apr 2026 -- The German Air Force has concluded its NATO enhanced Air Policing deployment at Mihail Kogălniceanu Air Base, Romania, after completing two consecutive rotations in support of the Alliance's south-eastern air defence mission. The German Eurofighter detachment hand over responsibility to the incoming Royal Air Force Typhoon detachment.

  • Sweden to supply Ukraine with advanced counter-Shahed systems: key facts about Tridon Mk2 Ukraine MOD 06 Apr 2026 -- The Defence Forces of Ukraine will receive advanced Tridon Mk2 air defence systems from Sweden. These systems will enhance Ukraine's air defence capabilities in countering Shahed drones and other aerial threats.

  • China-built engine fueled by hydrogen test-flown Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 06 Apr 2026 -- A 7.5-metric ton unmanned cargo aircraft powered by the AEP100, China's independently developed megawatt-class hydrogen-fueled turboprop engine, successfully completed its maiden flight on Saturday at an airport in Zhuzhou, Hunan province, marking the world's first test flight of a megawatt-class hydrogen-fueled aviation turboprop engine, according to China Daily.

  • Keel Laying of FPV-4 & Plate Cutting Ceremony for FPV-7 for Indian Coast Guard India PIB 06 Apr 2026 -- Keel laying ceremony for Fast Patrol Vessel-4 (FPV) (Yard 16504) along with plate cutting ceremony for FPV-7 (Yard 16507) of 14 FPV project (Yard 16501-14) for the Indian Coast Guard was held at Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL), Mumbai on 06 Apr 26.

  • Israel MOD to Accelerate Arrow Interceptor Production Israel MOD 06 Apr 2026 -- The Ministerial Committee for Procurement has approved the Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) plan for a major additional acceleration of Arrow interceptor production developed and manufactured at Israel Aerospace Industries (IAI).

  • JFC Naples Commander, at the MoND Headquarters Romania - Ministry of National Defence 06 Apr 2026 -- Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, and the Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad, had official meetings on Thursday, April 2, with Admiral George Wikoff, Commander of the Allied Joint Force Command Naples, at the Ministry of National Defence Headquarters.

  • PH Army, allies open 'Salaknib' Phase 1 drills Philippine News Agency 06 Apr 2026 -- The Exercise "Salaknib" Phase 1 featuring more than 7,000 Filipino, Japanese, American and Australian soldiers formally opened at Fort Magsaysay, Palayan City, Nueva Ecija on Monday.
  • PAF: Increased fighter jet sightings due to Cope Thunder drills Philippine News Agency 06 Apr 2026 -- The Philippine Air Force (PAF) on Monday reminded the public not to be alarmed by an increase in sightings of fighter jets flying from Basa and Clark Air Bases in Pampanga, noting that this is part of the ongoing Cope Thunder Exercise 2026 with its American counterparts.

  • Israel mulling 'unprecedented' collusion with Syria's HTS against Hezbollah: Report Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- A recent report has revealed that Syria's ruling Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham (HTS) militant group may join forces with Israel against the Lebanese Hezbollah resistance movement, now that the Tel Aviv regime has failed to materialize its dream of occupying southern Lebanon.

  • The Arab Republic of Egypt lauded on Monday the role of His Majesty King Mohammed VI, Chairman of the Al-Quds Committee, in supporting the resilience of the people of Al-Quds and safeguarding the Arab and Islamic cultural and religious identity of the city of Al-Quds, as well as its legal status, its civilizational significance, and its historical importance as a land of coexistence among the three religions. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 Apr 2026
  • MIKTA Statement on the Attack Against Indonesian UNIFIL Peacekeepers ROK MFA 06 Apr 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Ukraine UNOCHA 06 Apr 2026 -- UN continues to rush in aid as death toll nears 1,500

  • Senate eyes renewed push for PH-China oil exploration under PBBM Philippine News Agency 06 Apr 2026 -- The Senate is considering reviving joint oil exploration between the Philippines and China in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) under the administration of President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr., as the government looks for ways to secure alternative energy sources.
  • Think-tank not keen on PH-China joint energy exploration in WPS Philippine News Agency 06 Apr 2026 -- Think-tank Stratbase Institute is not in favor of any joint energy exploration deal between the Philippines and China in the West Philippine Sea (WPS).

  • The Arab Republic of Egypt reaffirmed on Monday in Cairo its support for the territorial integrity of the Kingdom of Morocco, as well as for the latest Security Council resolution and a consensual political solution. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 Apr 2026

News Reports

  • NASA's Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight NASA 06 Apr 2026 -- Editor's note: This release was updated at 7:28 p.m. EDT on April 6, 2026, to reflect Orion's final closest approach to the lunar surface from about 4,070 miles to about 4,067 miles. NASA also updated the farthest distance Orion will travel.
  • Artemis crew reaches the moon, approaches record-breaking distance from Earth Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 06 Apr 2026 -- The four astronauts of NASA's Artemis II mission entered the moon's gravitational sphere of influence early Monday morning as they cruised along a path that will soon take them over the shadowed, lunar far side to become the farthest-flying humans in history, Reuters reports.

  • UN80 Initiative enters 'delivery phase', as Member States review progress on key work areas UN News 06 Apr 2026 -- Top officials updated Member States Monday on selected proposals under the UN80 reform initiative, including an initial assessment of a possible merger between gender equality agency, UN Women, and the UN reproductive health agency, UNFPA, as well as updates on the technology and data tracks.

  • Meeting with Aeroflot CEO Sergei Aleksandrovsky President of Russia 06 Apr 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of the Management Board of Aeroflot - Russian Airlines Sergei Aleksandrovsky.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks at a meeting of the Russia-Islamic World strategic vision group with ambassadors of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation member states, Moscow, April 6, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 Apr 2026

  • Republican congressional delegation visits Taiwan, to meet President Lai CNA 06 Apr 2026 -- A Republican congressional delegation led by Zach Nunn, chair of the Republican Study Committee's (RSC) National Security Task Force, is visiting Taiwan this week and will meet with President Lai Ching-te (賴清德), the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) said Monday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 06 Apr 2026

  • Press Statement of Kim Yo Jong, Department Director of C.C., WPK KCNA 06 Apr 2026 -- Kim Yo Jong, department director of the Central Committee of the Workers' Party of Korea, issued the following press statement on April 6:
  • Measures Taken to Prevent Drought Damage KCNA 06 Apr 2026 -- As drought persists, farms in North Hwanghae Province of the DPRK are striving to minimize the drought damage to crops.

  • Expanded exchanges with France to boost creative, high-tech ties KOREA.net 06 Apr 2026 -- The state visit to Korea by French President Emmanuel Macron has fueled a rush to boost bilateral cooperation in a wider range of sectors.

  • Police arrest 235 elements affiliated with US, Israel in western Tehran Press TV 06 Apr 2026 -- Iranian police have successfully identified and arrested 235 elements affiliated with the US and Israel in western Tehran, 93 of whom were subsequently imprisoned according to court rulings.

  • Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies UN News 06 Apr 2026 -- The UN has issued an urgent call for international support as Cuba grapples with a 'worsening' humanitarian crisis fuelled by a prolonged energy blockade and the lingering devastation caused by Hurricane Melissa last year.

  • Hungary's Orban Visits Pipeline As Serbia Pushes Back On Claims Blaming Ukraine For Sabotage RFE/RL 06 Apr 2026 -- Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban traveled to the TurkStream gas pipeline on the Hungarian-Serbian border on April 6, inspecting infrastructure that his government says was the target of a foiled sabotage attempt -- six days before parliamentary elections that polls suggest he may lose.
  • Ukraine Might Be Behind TurkStream Sabotage Attempts - Orban Sputnik 06 Apr 2026 -- Last week, President of Serbia Alexandar Vucic told Orban that security forces had discovered explosive devices near a key gas pipeline linking Serbia and Hungary and carrying Russian gas via the TurkStream route.

  • President of Azerbaijan and Prime Minister of Georgia delivered press statements Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 06 Apr 2026 -- President of the Republic of Azerbaijan Ilham Aliyev and Prime Minister of Georgia Irakli Kobakhidze delivered press statements in Tbilisi on April 6.

  • World News in Brief: 'Skyrocketing' needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security UN News 06 Apr 2026 -- The UN is significantly scaling up its presence in the Sudanese capital, Khartoum, to expand life-saving operations as the conflict between rival militaries approaches its third year.
